jokes.text: I've had my skin tags removed since then so you can see my eyes now.
jokes.setup: Character casually mentions cosmetic procedure as improvement
jokes.punchline: Implication that skin tags completely obscured his eyes
jokes.explanation: Excellent character establishment - immediately tells us everything about Thad's awkwardness and complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Thank you, no everyone thinks of the young Nazi boy as the star of that show.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions he was the star of The Sound of Music
jokes.punchline: He played a Nazi child and thinks that's the starring role
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the casual Nazi reference combined with theatrical pretension is perfectly awkward

jokes.text: A lot of people didn't realize that was the free, unlicensed version.
jokes.setup: Context of amateur college theater production
jokes.punchline: They couldn't afford the rights to The Sound of Music
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing the low-budget nature of their college theater

jokes.text: I don't see people that look like that.
jokes.setup: Kenneth appears to be talking to himself
jokes.punchline: He literally doesn't perceive unattractive people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdism - his selective vision is both innocent and horrifying

jokes.text: And this is coming from someone who wrote lyrics to the song the cantina band plays in Star Wars.
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness of her own extreme nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Great Liz characterization - her complete lack of self-awareness about her own nerdiness

jokes.text: No, that was sad Thad the skintag lad. I coined that.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if this is the billionaire Thad
jokes.punchline: Liz's terrible nickname and pride in creating it
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Liz's cruel streak and terrible poetry

jokes.text: I don't know a lot about business, but he did an Internet and now the computers like him and Wall Street is Google.
jokes.punchline: Complete misunderstanding of technology and finance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna moment - the phrase 'he did an Internet' is comedy gold, perfectly capturing her vapid misunderstanding

jokes.text: Liz, I'm on a sexual walkabout right now.
jokes.setup: Jenna wants to meet the billionaire
jokes.punchline: Misusing 'walkabout' in a sexual context
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment - appropriating spiritual terms for shallow purposes

jokes.text: Poke your eye, pull your hair, you forgot what clothes to wear.
jokes.setup: Liz not wearing yellow and blue
jokes.punchline: Made-up Leap Day tradition/punishment rhyme
jokes.explanation: Good world-building joke establishing the absurd Leap Day mythology

jokes.text: He lives in the Mariana trench. He emerges every four years to trade children's tears for candy?
jokes.punchline: Disturbing fairy tale involving children's tears
jokes.explanation: Excellent world-building - creates a genuinely creepy mythological figure with perfect Kenneth innocence

jokes.text: After high school, I went on rumspringa. It was crazy. I totally snuck into The Witches of Eastwick.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her wild Amish heritage
jokes.punchline: Her idea of rebellion was sneaking into a PG-13 movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz characterization - her sheltered background making even minor rebellion seem wild to her

jokes.text: Certainly not at a Michael's crafts crafting cruise.
jokes.setup: Jack asking where she was four years ago
jokes.punchline: Admitting to the lamest possible vacation
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz's sad lifestyle choices

jokes.text: I listened to rap music for the first time. Not a fan.
jokes.punchline: Polite dismissal of entire music genre
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth beat - his polite, innocent racism/classism

jokes.text: Okay, fine, I'll go, but if he has a giant chandelier in his house, I'm out. Those things fall.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to go to Thad's party
jokes.punchline: Paranoid and oddly specific concern about chandeliers
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz neurosis - finding danger in the most mundane rich person trapping

jokes.text: We just, uh, stomp on your foot. Kick you in the knee, Yankees suck, go pats.
jokes.punchline: Escalating violence ending in sports rivalry
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke playing on Boston stereotypes

jokes.text: I can't believe the woman who watches all six pawn shop reality shows has never seen the movie, Leap Dave Williams.
jokes.punchline: Specific number of terrible shows vs. made-up Leap Day movie
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Liz's trashy TV habits vs. missing fake culture

jokes.text: Dave, is everything all right? I definitely don't have gills. I mean, nothing. I mean, yes.
jokes.setup: Clip from fake Leap Day movie
jokes.punchline: Terrible dialogue denying having gills
jokes.explanation: Good fake movie parody - the terrible dialogue and obvious exposition

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that's what Chris kept quoting this morning during our love... making.
jokes.punchline: Awkward revelation about boyfriend's movie quotes during sex
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - oversharing about weird sexual behavior

jokes.text: He's a mormon. You know how they are about Leap Day.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Gabe has the week off
jokes.punchline: Implying Mormons have strong Leap Day beliefs
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist world-building - creating fake religious observances

jokes.text: Of course, take off my bald cap. Not put on my wig.
jokes.setup: Jack telling Kenneth to take off his bald cap
jokes.punchline: Kenneth wearing a bald cap over his actual hair
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual absurdity - Kenneth's backwards understanding of costume logic

jokes.text: You know, the one that crosses so slowly across the road and then you swerve to make sure you hit it and then a car coming the other way swerves to avoid you and goes off a cliff. And then that night you and your companion have the greatest sex of your lives because you're both sharing a secret.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate murder fantasy ending in arousal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation - starts innocent then becomes deeply disturbing psychological profile

jokes.text: My aquarium guy's coming to see about turning this whole room into an aquarium. Who's really in the aquarium? The fish or us?
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-philosophical question about aquarium rooms
jokes.explanation: Good rich weirdo characterization - pointless expensive renovation with fake depth

jokes.text: Mean laughter, sound of a drink being poured. What are you writing now, you slack-jawed donkey?
jokes.punchline: Jack dictating stage directions and insults into his message
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke - Jack literally dictating the subtext and breaking the fourth wall

jokes.text: Right, I did an ad and insisted on being paid in beni bucks.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's terrible business decision to take gift cards as payment
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy characterization - his terrible financial decisions and made-up terminology

jokes.text: It's like I said in my cameo appearance in Leap Dave Williams, 'gimme your wallet, old man!'
jokes.punchline: His movie cameo was just mugging someone
jokes.explanation: Good callback that subverts expectation - his 'inspirational' quote was criminal

jokes.text: Help us to 'Whip' the competition.
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally racist corporate slogan
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe comedy about corporate tone-deafness

jokes.text: You know the saying, 'rhubarb red, eat away, rhubarb green, don't eat them.'
jokes.punchline: Made-up folk wisdom that doesn't scan properly
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth beat - his fake folksy wisdom with poor rhyme scheme

jokes.text: I've watched newt gingrich eat a plate of ribs. I think my stomach can handle some rhubarb leaves.
jokes.punchline: Jack's disgusting political observation as strength proof
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line combining political awareness with grotesque imagery

jokes.text: One of my birds is sick and I want to get home in time to baptize her.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking how long he'll be needed
jokes.punchline: Baptizing a pet bird
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth absurdity - treating pets like humans in religious contexts

jokes.text: Just dab some scotch on your neck and make your eyes the color of a winter crystal so she'll think you're me.
jokes.setup: Jack telling nanny to impersonate him
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific instructions for gender-swapped impersonation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack absurdity - his narcissistic assumption that anyone can become him with the right accessories

jokes.text: Frak, I'm supposed to say 'floor' first.
jokes.punchline: Thad's embarrassment over messing up his own tech demo
jokes.explanation: Good tech bro failure moment - the gap between vision and execution

jokes.text: Watching you every night on that stage and realizing my dad was wrong. I do like girls.
jokes.setup: Thad confessing his love for Liz
jokes.punchline: His sexual awakening happened watching her in college theater
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the deeply personal overshare combined with theater nerddom

jokes.text: In Game of Thrones, Xaro is a rich merchant prince who wishes to acquaint himself with the mother of dragons.
jokes.setup: Explaining why his company is called Xaro
jokes.punchline: Named his company after a character trying to seduce Daenerys
jokes.explanation: Clever nerd reference showing Thad's elaborate romantic scheming through fantasy literature

jokes.text: Great, well... Scooby Doo!
jokes.punchline: Liz's complete panic expressed as random cartoon reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect panic response - the complete non-sequitur showing Liz's brain breaking under pressure

jokes.text: Well the joke's on you because that commercial never aired for unexplained reasons.
jokes.setup: Being told he did the ad for nothing
jokes.punchline: Tracy's twisted logic about 'winning' when his ad was rejected
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - finding victory in obvious failure

jokes.text: I just touched the table again. God, you call for help! I'll just put my head on the table for a second.
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of being too full to function
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit - decent execution but predictable

jokes.text: Nothing that happens on Leap Day counts.
jokes.punchline: Universal excuse that Leap Day absolves all consequences
jokes.explanation: Strong running gag that drives multiple plot threads - becomes philosophical about consequences

jokes.text: It looks like we've got a slut-off on our hands.
jokes.punchline: Jenna's crude term for romantic rivalry
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna vulgarity - her unique way of describing competition

jokes.text: One Leap Day when I was a kid, the Harlem River froze, and I decided to cross it carrying my brick collection.
jokes.punchline: Carrying a brick collection across frozen river
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy backstory - the casual mention of collecting bricks as a child

jokes.text: I have screen grabs of all your nip s.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of creepy behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor about creepy fan behavior

jokes.text: Click click. That's their stilettos. Click click.
jokes.punchline: Sound effects for stiletto heels
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Jenna's dramatic flair

jokes.text: I've dug too many graves.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission to grave digging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth darkness - the innocent delivery of disturbing confession

jokes.text: I do have a long, elegant face, thank you, but I can't enjoy your compliment, because I'm sad.
jokes.setup: Old man asking why the long face
jokes.punchline: Tracy taking the idiom literally but politely
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy beat - his narcissism interpreting everything as compliment

jokes.text: I am always coughing up blood. Could that be Leap Day William trying to get out?
jokes.setup: Old man explaining Leap Day William lives inside us
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's concerning medical symptom as spiritual explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy combining Kenneth's health issues with mythology

jokes.text: I am always coughing up blood. Could that be Leap Day William trying to get out?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Leap Day William being inside us
jokes.punchline: Tracy's medical condition reframed as spiritual
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - serious medical issue treated as mystical experience

jokes.text: What are you? An intercourse monster?
jokes.punchline: Liz's prudish way of saying 'sex fiend'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz prudishness - her inability to say 'sex' even when angry

jokes.text: Is sex like when the Na'vi intertwine their hair braids?
jokes.punchline: Comparing sex to Avatar movie aliens
jokes.explanation: Good virgin nerd characterization - understanding sex only through sci-fi

jokes.text: Aw, damn it. Hot bitches.
jokes.setup: Models arriving to compete for Thad
jokes.punchline: Liz's defeated reaction in uncharacteristic language
jokes.explanation: Good character break - Liz using crude language shows her frustration

jokes.text: Jackie, we can't go to the parade today. I got called into work... at the hospital... where I'm a nurse.
jokes.setup: Young Jack's mother canceling Leap Day plans
jokes.punchline: Obviously lying about being a nurse
jokes.explanation: Backstory moment showing origin of Jack's trust issues with transparent lie

jokes.text: Okay, I'll change me eye color, but it seem like a waste of voodoo.
jokes.setup: Sherry the nanny following Jack's instructions
jokes.punchline: Treating eye color change as voodoo magic
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - treating mundane task as magical

jokes.text: Looks like this time the male will be in the Czech.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'check' and her nationality
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly obvious nationality pun

jokes.text: Hey, Liz, a gym teacher on a sex tour of Indonesia called. He wants his shirt back.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult about her clothing
jokes.explanation: Creative elaborate insult with specific absurd scenario

jokes.text: Looks like this time the male will be in the Czech.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'check' and her nationality
jokes.explanation: Weak pun - predictable nationality-based wordplay

jokes.text: Remember where I came from. But we all came from the sea. Sea? Like the letter 'C', which is part of the alphabet. Alphabet soup. Soup kitchen.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to figure out what to do
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness leading to charity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy logic chain - his bizarre but somehow functional thought process

jokes.text: I saved Leap Day! And connected with my son! And I solved the big case from earlier!
jokes.punchline: Claiming to solve non-existent case
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy grandiosity - claiming credit for solving imaginary problems

jokes.text: But, these lessons aren't good just for every four years. No. They're good every year. Because we should live every day as if it's Leap Day, and every Leap Day as if it's your last.
jokes.punchline: Fake profound wisdom about living like it's Leap Day
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke - parodying sitcom moral lessons with nonsensical wisdom

jokes.text: No. They're good every year. Because we should live every day as if it's Leap Day, and every Leap Day as if it's your last.
jokes.punchline: Parody of inspirational sayings with absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Good parody of sitcom morals with Kenneth's earnest delivery of nonsense
